creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents lack sufficient context and memory between interactions, reducing their practical value despite individual capability. He uses the analogy of a genius with amnesia to illustrate how limited scope and state loss prevent agents from delivering real value on substantial codebases.

videos.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
videos.summary: Nishant Gupta from Meta presents production evaluation strategies for autonomous AI agents that move beyond static offline benchmarks. The talk addresses continuous evaluation pipelines integrated into production, covering tool use assessment, reasoning validation, drift detection, and human-in-the-loop systems for real-world agentic workflows.

videos.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
videos.summary: Jacob E. Thomas argues that current persona-evaluation benchmarks systematically fail to detect "Miranda distortion," where culturally dominant representations in training data overwhelm primary documentary records, causing character simulations to produce convincing but anachronistic outputs. He proposes a framework shifting from cognitive simulation to epistemic simulation with historian-in-the-loop evaluation, validated through the pre-registered Prism Experiment testing historical figure reasoning across documented temporal moments.

videos.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
videos.summary: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift engineering focus from writing individual lines of code to designing agentic systems at a higher level. Traditional engineering principles like systems thinking, decomposition, and state management remain valuable and necessary in an AI-native world.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses Google's playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model comprises only 10% of development success while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) comprises 90%. The video explores the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, context engineering as a core skill, and the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers use self-prompting agent loops where orchestrators spawn parallel worker agents instead of manually prompting agents. The loop architecture stores runs in a database with observability dashboards, enabling hands-off agentic systems that can run for extended periods.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is an open-source meta-harness that orchestrates multiple AI coding agents as interchangeable workers. It enables workflows where different models handle different tasks, such as Claude for implementation and Codex for code review, with human-in-the-loop governance and cross-device session support.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the withdrawal of Claude Fable 5 following a US government export-control directive allegedly related to a jailbreak vulnerability. The video presents this as a regulatory intervention and compares Fable 5's capabilities and pricing against Opus 4.8.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses OpenClaw Creator's new secret project, providing a GitHub repository for setting up Crabbox and Codebase harness through the AI Builder Club. The project includes Superdesign, a development tool available at superdesign.dev.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason presents Loop Engineer as a practical setup guide, offering a GitHub repository with a codebase harness and knowledge template, plus a workshop through AI Builder Club for learners to build AI agents.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: A video tutorial on building proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems. Covers proactive AI loops, autonomous optimization use cases for ads and SEO, and the enabling technologies like Loopany, memory systems, and tools.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and mission exploration capabilities in coding agents, comparing them to Ralph-loop 2.0 and positioning these as steps toward true autonomous coders. The video covers setup, prompting techniques, and goal-buddy functionality.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT explained visually..
videos.summary: A visual tutorial breaking down how Generative Pretrained Transformers work, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed forward networks, positional embeddings, and residual connections. The content explains the basic mechanics underlying leading models like Gemini, Opus, and Qwen.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents a 10-minute explanation of Andrej Karpathy's Bigram Language Model, covering tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, and training with optimizers. The video serves as a foundational tutorial for understanding how language models process tokens before advancing to attention mechanisms and GPT.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 series models in Nano, Super, and Ultra variants featuring hybrid Mamba transformer, MTP, and Latent MoE architectures. The video analyzes how these architectural components combine to differentiate Nemotron 3 from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a language model that introduces MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA), shifting from full attention to sparse attention. The model includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache reading to reduce computational overhead during prefill and decode stages of inference.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Tool calling is the mechanism by which LLM-based agents request and execute external tools to access real-time data and take actions beyond language generation. The agent requests a tool invocation through its system prompt, the framework executes the tool, and the loop continues, fundamentally distinguishing agents from stateless chatbots.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a tool that equips AI coding agents with access to up-to-date AWS documentation, best practices, and tested procedures. It provides knowledge and guardrails to improve agent success rates and reduce wasted effort when working with AWS services.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS tutorial on running Claude Code through Amazon Bedrock to keep data within AWS infrastructure. Covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and guardrails for governance.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job hunting strategies in an AI-screening environment, emphasizing networking, personal brand building, and the enduring value of junior developers. The content argues that jobs evolve rather than disappear due to AI, and provides practical advice for career development including open source contributions and building projects.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube video examining whether Google engineers engage in informal code generation practices. It compares OpenAI Codex and Claude Code, reviews Google's approach in 2024-2026, and discusses the prevalence of "vibing" or informal coding across the industry.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or tool positioned as disruptive to open source maintainers. The video explores tensions between commercial interests and open source sustainability, framed as a debate about whether this entity is harmful to the open source model.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that software advantage depends on customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity rather than code alone. He contends that the predicted "SaaSpocalypse" from AI is exaggerated and that building products that create raving fans is key to business success.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that the near-term robotics race focused on humanoid forms may be myopic, and that designing robots for delight and human interaction rather than utility alone will be crucial as billions of robots enter society. She contends the real challenge for robotics is not achieving intelligence but thoughtfully integrating robots into human life.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder and co-CEO of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against legacy giants by consolidating finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data. She explains why even Google relied on spreadsheets for strategic planning, her decision to build a full enterprise platform from day one, and the trust-based growth strategies that won over customers like Figma, Brex, and Anthropic despite being a Paris-based startup with zero US brand awareness.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that genuine product-market fit manifests as customer pull (demand for pricing) rather than push (passive interest), and that founders should avoid relying on formal interviews in favor of embedded customer relationships. He emphasizes the importance of betting on where AI models are heading rather than where they currently stand.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that businesses have overlooked critical security threats posed by agentic AI, including prompt injection attacks, unintended data deletion, and unauthorized system actions. Despite AI's potential, these risks have caused widespread business hesitation around deployment.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: This Is Tech Year podcast episode hosted by Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's latest developments with guest Joao. The episode appears to tie together tech news with cultural context around Lisbon and the Santos festival.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof compares small agile teams to large teams, arguing that smaller units achieve greater task completion while larger teams lose efficiency to excessive meetings. The view emphasizes that organizational size directly impacts productivity and meeting culture.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: A short-form video discussing how AI demand is driving growth in high-bandwidth memory from manufacturers Micron and SK Hynix. The content predicts major growth ahead for these companies in the memory stack market.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
